qa_pairs.question: What is the nature of this document?
qa_pairs.answer: This document outlines Compute Canada policies.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key policies listed by Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: The key policies include Policy Definitions, Terms of Use, Access Policy, Privacy Policy, Security Policy, Information Security Management System Policy, Resource Allocation Competition General Policy (Draft), and Scratch purging policy.

qa_pairs.question: Is the Resource Allocation Competition General Policy a final document?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Resource Allocation Competition General Policy is currently a Draft.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Terms of Use for Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find the Terms of Use at https://www.computecanada.ca/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/Terms-of-Use.pdf.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I access the Access Policy?
qa_pairs.answer: The Access Policy is available at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/accessing-resources/access-policy/.

qa_pairs.question: What is the link to the Compute Canada Privacy Policy?
qa_pairs.answer: The Privacy Policy can be found at https://www.computecanada.ca/privacy-policy/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I read the Security Policy?
qa_pairs.answer: The Security Policy is located at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/information-security/security-policy/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information regarding the Information Security Management System Policy?
qa_pairs.answer: Details on the Information Security Management System Policy are available at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/information-security/isms-policy/.

qa_pairs.question: What is the official website for Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: The official Compute Canada Website is https://www.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I access CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: You can access CCDB at https://ccdb.computecanada.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a technical glossary available for Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the Compute Canada Technical Glossary is available at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/accessing-resources/glossary/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information on applying for an account?
qa_pairs.answer: Information on applying for an account can be found at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/account-management/apply-for-an-account/.

qa_pairs.question: How do I renew my Compute Canada account?
qa_pairs.answer: Information on renewing an account is available at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/account-management/account-renewals/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I learn about creating a cloud account with Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: You can learn about creating a cloud account at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/national-services/compute-canada-cloud/.

qa_pairs.question: How do I access Compute Canada Resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Information on accessing resources (default, RAS, RAC) is available at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/accessing-resources/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find details about the Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Details on the Resource Allocation Competition are provided at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/accessing-resources/resource-allocation-competitions/.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a guide for submitting a Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a Canadian Common CV (CCV) Submission Guide is available at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/account-management/ccv/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find guidelines for acknowledging Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: Guidelines for acknowledging Compute Canada are provided at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/accessing-resources/acknowledging-compute-canada/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I get an overview of technical support for Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: A Technical Support Overview can be found at https://www.computecanada.ca/research-portal/technical-support/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information about Compute Canada's Regional Partners?
qa_pairs.answer: Information about Regional Partners is available at https://www.computecanada.ca/about/partners/.

qa_pairs.question: What is the address for Compute Canada's Technical Documentation?
qa_pairs.answer: The Technical Documentation is located at https://docs.computecanada.ca (docs.computecanada.ca).
